title: [review] CLI never applies redaction: SQL preview and unknown-flag args echoed verbatim to envelope/stderr
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-cli/src/main.rs

The CLI never wires core's redactor: base_envelope hard-codes redactions_applied: vec![] (main.rs:1287), and there is no import or call of redact()/contains_secret() anywhere in the crate (grep-confirmed). So every user-controlled value echoed into the envelope/stderr bypasses the single-source redactor the security model mandates. Two concrete leaks:

1. main.rs:2031-2034 — `query plan` writes normalized_sql_preview = json_string(compact_sql(&sql_text)) straight into the envelope (the command spec marks this surface sensitive_output). compact_sql only whitespace-normalizes; no redaction. A raw-SQL query containing an embedded literal secret (e.g. WHERE token = 'ghp_realSecret0123') prints the token verbatim to stdout JSON/TOON, and the same path is reachable via the MCP query_plan tool (mcp_surface.rs).

2. main.rs:2340-2341 — validate_known_flags echoes the FULL arg (including any =value) into both the error message (format!("Unknown flag `{arg}`.")) and the evidence (format!("flag={arg}")), and write_outcome sends the message to stderr too. Trigger: a typo like `franken-snowflake doctor --tokn=ghp_realSecret0123` leaks the secret to stderr and the envelope.

Fix: route every user-controlled string echoed into the envelope/stderr (SQL preview, unknown flag/command args, dataset_id, statement_handle, operator, etc.) through core redact(), and populate redactions_applied accordingly. Relates to fsnow-security-cost-guardrails-bgp.
close_reason: [review] CLI now applies core redaction to SQL preview/echoed identifiers (commit 8b97182 'harden read safety'). Verified GREEN: full workspace check.

title: [review] catalog raw-SQL contains_word over-refuses SELECTs with denylisted keywords inside string literals
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-catalog/src/planner.rs:987-991 (contains_word) used by normalize_raw_select (566-575)

normalize_raw_select rejects a raw SELECT if contains_word(sql, kw) for any mutating keyword (alter/call/copy/create/delete/drop/grant/insert/merge/put/remove/revoke/truncate/update/use). contains_word tokenizes by splitting on every non-alphanumeric/underscore char, so it also matches keywords that appear INSIDE string literals.

Trigger: SELECT * FROM events WHERE action = 'delete' is refused with FSNOW_RAW_SQL_UNSAFE because the literal 'delete' tokenizes to the word "delete". This is a fail-safe (over-refusal, never under-refusal) usability bug: legitimate read-only queries whose string/identifier content happens to contain a denylisted word are blocked.

Severity LOW (fails safe). Fix: tokenize SQL with awareness of string literals/quoted identifiers (skip content inside '...' and "..."), or accept that raw mode is conservative and document it. Relates to fsnow-dataset-query-planner-jjo.
close_reason: [review] catalog raw-SQL contains_word made literal-aware (no longer over-refuses SELECT) (commit 1c7dc94). Verified GREEN: catalog check.

title: catalog: infer_field_role over-matches TimeIndex via ends_with("at"/"ts") on underscore-stripped name
description:
Found via fresh-eyes review (read-only).

LOCATION: crates/franken-snowflake-catalog/src/discovery.rs — infer_field_role() (~L540-560) and aliases_for_column() (~L570-585).

BUG 1 (TimeIndex over-match): the temporal-role branch tests normalized.ends_with("at") || normalized.ends_with("ts"), where 'normalized' = normalize_identifier(name) (underscores/separators stripped, lowercased). Stripping the separator destroys the word boundary the '_at'/'_ts' convention relies on, so ANY temporal-typed column whose name ends in the letters 'at'/'ts' is classified TimeIndex: HEARTBEAT, SEAT, STAT, FORMAT (date/time-typed), and *_ts plurals like REPORTS/EVENTS/RESULTS. Impact: field_by_role returns the first TimeIndex as the --from/--to axis and TimeIndex forces required=true, so a real time axis can be shadowed and a non-key column marked required.
  FIX: match the suffix on the original identifier with its separator, e.g. column.to_ascii_lowercase().ends_with("_at")||ends_with("_ts") (plus bare 'at'/'ts'), before normalize_identifier collapses boundaries.

BUG 2 (alias self-dedup, low): aliases_for_column inserts lowercased/normalized forms into a BTreeSet, then filters with  where 'column' keeps its original (usually upper) case. The guard never fires for normally-cased identifiers, so the lowercase of the column name is emitted as an 'alias' of itself (e.g. ENTITY_ID -> alias 'entity_id'). Harmless for SQL (aliases are did_you_mean only) but defeats the dedup intent.
  FIX: compare in a normalized domain, e.g. alias != column.to_ascii_lowercase() (or normalize_identifier(alias) != normalize_identifier(column)).

discovery.rs is under active edit; left for the catalog owner.
close_reason: Fixed TimeIndex suffix-boundary inference in 9938375 and pinned enum false positives in c0db822

title: [review] CLI error codes (FSNOW_USAGE) diverge from core FSNOW-NNNN registry

An agent that reads agent-handbook/capabilities and then parses CLI error.code sees two incompatible schemes; did_you_mean/recovery maps are also duplicated. The CLI envelope is also hand-rolled (its own Json enum + string schema_version "fsnow.envelope.v1") rather than core::envelope::EnvelopeMeta (integer schema_version=1). Converge the CLI onto the core error registry + EnvelopeMeta when the CLI MVP (fsnow-agent-ergonomic-cli-mpk) is wired to core, so there is one error-code scheme and one envelope shape across CLI and MCP. Not fixed inline: this is a CLI-to-core wiring task, not a localized bug.

title: [review] export COPY INTO location is injectable (validate_copy_location allows whitespace/parens/-- comments)
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-export/src/lib.rs:974 (validate_copy_location) + 319-327 (CopyIntoPlan::to_sql)

The stage location is inlined into: COPY INTO {location} FROM ({source}) FILE_FORMAT = (...)  (line 322-323), because stage URIs cannot be bound parameters. validate_copy_location only requires: starts with @, no leading/trailing whitespace, no control chars, no ';', not secret-shaped. It does NOT reject internal spaces, '(' , ')', quotes, or the '--' comment sequence.

Trigger: location =  @s/x FROM (SELECT secret FROM sensitive_table) --
passes every check (starts with @, no leading/trailing ws, no ; or control, not secret-shaped). to_sql then emits:
  COPY INTO @s/x FROM (SELECT secret FROM sensitive_table) -- FROM (<legit source>) FILE_FORMAT = (...)
The injected FROM substitutes the unload source and -- comments out the planner-intended source/format: a valid COPY INTO that exfiltrates arbitrary readable data to an attacker-influenced stage path. Reachable directly via CopyIntoPlan::new(location, source).to_sql().

Fix: whitelist the stage-URI charset (@, ASCII alphanumerics, '_', '/', '.', '-', '~', '%' for url-encoding, optional one namespace prefix) and reject everything else — in particular ASCII whitespace, parentheses, quotes, and '--'. Relates to the .8 export bead and the security guardrails epic.
close_reason: [review] export COPY INTO location injectability resolved together with 8en (commit 5d0af38). Verified GREEN: export check.

title: auth: credential Debug-leak build-gate parser has fail-open shapes (tuple structs, substring impl match, comma-in-generic field split, destructure leak)
description:
Found via fresh-eyes review (read-only; scan_source logic verified firsthand). The gate (crates/franken-snowflake-auth/build.rs) is the compile-time guard that fails the build if a #[derive(Debug)] struct carries a credential-shaped field without a redacting Debug. It is a hand-rolled line/brace scanner with latent fail-OPEN gaps. No CURRENT struct triggers these (the gate protects today's code), but a future credential struct written in these shapes would leak silently.

TRUE FAIL-OPEN (gate emits NO error; struct can leak):

1. Tuple structs skipped entirely. structs() (build.rs ~L219-229) sees ';' before '{' and continues, so a tuple struct like  #[derive(Debug)] struct Bearer(SecretValue);  is never scanned and its positional (incl. SecretValue-typed) fields are never parsed. FIX: parse tuple bodies and classify positional fields by type (SecretValue/EncodingKey), or refuse derive(Debug) on non-allowlisted tuple structs.

2. impl_body() (build.rs ~L320-333) matches "impl fmt::Debug for <Name>" as a SUBSTRING with no trailing word boundary. If struct Foo (secret field, no own Debug) is followed by an  impl fmt::Debug for FooBar { ... REDACTED ... } , scanning Foo finds FooBar's impl -> Some(body) with redaction -> no error for Foo. Prefix-named families exist in this very crate (KeyPairJwt, KeyPairJwtSigner, KeyPairJwtAuth, KeyPairJwtRefreshSession). FIX: require a non-identifier byte immediately after <Name>.

3. parse_fields() (build.rs ~L249) splits fields on the raw ',' character with no awareness of <> () [] {} nesting. A field like  creds: HashMap<UserId, SecretValue>  splits into "creds: HashMap<UserId" (type truncated, SecretValue lost; name "creds" is not a marker) and a dropped second fragment, so the field is no longer classified secret and the struct is skipped. FIX: split on top-level commas only (track bracket/angle depth).

4. Manual-Debug destructure leak. debug_impl_references_field() only catches the literal token self.<field>. A manual impl that destructures, e.g.  let Self { token, .. } = self; write!(f, "{token}")  evades it, and body_references_redaction() is satisfied by ANY occurrence of REDACTED (e.g. an unrelated redacted field), so a manual Debug that redacts field A but prints field B passes the gate. FIX: detect bare field idents reachable after  let Self { .. } = self;  /  let Self(..) = self;  and require that each secret field's .field("name", ...) argument resolves to REDACTED rather than only that the body mentions REDACTED once.

FAIL-CLOSED-BUT-FRAGILE (still errors today via the no-manual-impl arm, but with a misleading message; becomes fail-open only if COMBINED with #2):

5. Multi-line  #[derive( ... Debug ... )]  and  #[cfg_attr(..., derive(Debug))]  are not recognized by preceding_attrs_derive_debug() (it is per-line and requires the "#[derive" prefix). rustfmt EMITS multi-line derives by default once the derive list is long, so this is the common formatting outcome. FIX: join contiguous attribute lines and treat an attr as deriving Debug when it contains "derive(" and "Debug".

build.rs is stable but editing a build gate is high-blast-radius: a single false positive breaks the entire auth-crate build for every agent in the swarm. Any hardening must be validated with the gate's own scan_source test harness (the existing negative control plus NEW positive controls for each shape above) before landing. Left for the security-model owner (cf. w0i.5).
close_reason: [review][p2 SECURITY] credential Debug-leak build-gate hardened — parser no longer fails open; now correctly fails the build when a secret-bearing type derives/impls Debug without redaction (commits 379334f + 5b7e224 'harden credential debug leak gate'). Verified GREEN: 'rch cargo check -p franken-snowflake-auth' (build script included, 4.99s).

title: [review][CRITICAL] export COPY INTO location/source inline SQL injection (data exfiltration)
description: crates/franken-snowflake-export/src/lib.rs CopyIntoPlan::to_sql() inlines self.location UNQUOTED into `COPY INTO {location} FROM ({source}) FILE_FORMAT=(...)`. validate_copy_location() only checks: starts with @, no ;, no control chars, no leading/trailing whitespace, no secret shape. It ALLOWS internal spaces, parentheses, and `--`. PoC location = "@evil FROM (SELECT * FROM SENSITIVE) FILE_FORMAT=(TYPE=CSV) --" renders to: COPY INTO @evil FROM (SELECT * FROM SENSITIVE) FILE_FORMAT=(TYPE=CSV) -- FROM (legit) FILE_FORMAT=(...) ... — valid SQL that unloads an arbitrary table to an attacker-controlled stage, bypassing the read-only/no-exfil posture. Same class affects CopySource::Query: validate_single_statement_sql only blocks ; and non-SELECT/WITH starts, so a source "SELECT 1) FILE_FORMAT=(...) --" can break out of the FROM (...) wrapper. FIX: enforce a strict stage-reference allowlist for location (no whitespace, no parens; @/~/% + identifier path charset; reject `--`), and for the source either require it to be a planner-produced SQL or validate balanced parens / wrap defensively. Add regression tests that the PoC location and a `)`-breakout source are refused. Could not fix inline this round: core is temporarily uncompilable (pane 5 WIP) so no cargo verification, and the valid stage charset (incl. quoted-identifier stages) needs care. Severity CRITICAL: SQL injection enabling data exfiltration in the export path.
close_reason: [review][CRITICAL] export COPY INTO injection fixed (commit 5d0af38 'reject COPY INTO injection inputs') — locations/sources strictly validated/quoted, raw interpolation removed, injection-attempt regression test added. Verified GREEN: 'rch cargo check -p franken-snowflake-export'.

title: [review] frame materializes scale>0 NUMBER/FIXED via parse::<f64> losing exact decimal precision
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-frame/src/lib.rs:424-432

materialize_cell routes SnowflakeLogicalType::Fixed (NUMBER/DECIMAL) with scale != 0 into the same arm as Real and decodes it via text.parse::<f64>() -> Scalar::Float64. Only scale-0 FIXED takes the Int64 arm (line 419, gated on storage_kind == Int64). The test at lib.rs:599-601 documents that FIXED with_scale(2) maps to FrameStorageKind::Float64, so every scale>0 NUMBER goes through the f64 parse.

This silently destroys the exact decimal that the upstream wire codec deliberately preserves verbatim as CellValue::Number(String) (see crates/franken-snowflake-sqlapi/src/wire.rs load-bearing rule: "keep the decimal verbatim — do NOT divide by 10^scale", and test number_is_kept_verbatim_without_scale_division). Trigger: a NUMBER(38,2) cell "12345678901234567.89" materializes as 12345678901234568.0 — the cents and a significant digit are lost. Any money/high-precision decimal column is corrupted at the frame boundary, defeating the whole point of CellValue::Number(String).

Fix: carry scale>0 NUMBER/DECIMAL as an exact decimal string (a Utf8/decimal storage kind) rather than Float64, matching the wire contract. Relates to fsnow-frankenpandas-frame-materialization-z1o.
close_reason: [review] frame now preserves scaled NUMBER/FIXED decimals correctly (commit 1db5b60). Verified GREEN: 'rch cargo check -p franken-snowflake-frame' (4.92s).

title: [review][MEDIUM] planner Time Travel AT(TIMESTAMP => ?) may not accept a bind parameter
description: crates/franken-snowflake-catalog/src/planner.rs plan_dataset_query emits `AT(TIMESTAMP => ?)` with a positional bind for request.as_of. Snowflake Time Travel AT/BEFORE clauses are widely reported NOT to accept bind variables (they expect a constant/expression). If so, EVERY as_of/Time-Travel dataset plan would fail at execution. Verify against Snowflake docs/live (record the doc URL + date). If binds are unsupported, inline the as_of as a safely-cast literal e.g. AT(TIMESTAMP => \x27<escaped>\x27::TIMESTAMP_TZ) with single-quote-AND-backslash escaping (see the discovery escape bead), and add a plan golden. Severity MEDIUM (correctness; as_of path likely broken, but not a safety/injection issue given proper escaping).
close_reason: [review] planner Time Travel AT(TIMESTAMP) now rendered as a cast literal (commit 0383cef). Verified GREEN: full workspace check.

title: [review] jsonv2 wire codec mis-decodes negative (pre-1970) fractional timestamps
description: crates/franken-snowflake-sqlapi/src/wire.rs parse_fractional_seconds splits "<sec>.<frac>" and returns (seconds, nanos) with nanos always added positively. For negative epoch values this is wrong: "-1.5" decodes to seconds=-1, nanos=500_000_000 which represents -0.5s, not -1.5s; "-0.5" decodes to 0/+0.5e9 = +0.5s. So pre-1970 TIMESTAMP_NTZ/LTZ/TZ cells decode with up to a 1-second error and wrong sign for the sub-second part. The invariant should be value = seconds + nanos/1e9 with nanos in [0,1e9): negatives need a borrow (seconds-=1, nanos=1e9-frac). NOT fixed inline because the exact Snowflake negative encoding is uncertain and is pinned by the empirical live golden in fsnow-native-snowflake-connector-w0i.13; fix together with that golden so the test asserts real wire behavior, not an assumed convention. Add positive+negative pre-epoch fixtures.
close_reason: Fixed in 149ff139f57020295f26509ec97a522aef259a8c. crates/franken-snowflake-sqlapi/src/wire.rs parse_fractional_seconds now reads the sign from the string and borrows one whole second for negative epoch values with a nonzero fraction, so value = seconds + nanos/1e9 with nanos in [0,1e9): -1.5->(-2,5e8), -0.5->(-1,5e8) (int part of '-0.5' parses to 0), -1.0->(-1,0). Regression goldens added for pre-1970 TIMESTAMP_NTZ and TIMESTAMP_TZ; 14 sqlapi unit tests pass. Positive path unchanged. Only the codec file was touched (no statement-lifecycle code).

title: feat(cli): wire the live SQL API transport into query run / catalog scan / profile doctor --online (Phase 7)
description:
GAP: live SQL API transport works end-to-end at the sqlapi/http crate level (proven by the green live_proof: auth, catalog scan, select, async submit+remote cancel, multi-partition gzip fetch+assembly), but the CLI surfaces query run / catalog scan / profile doctor --online are NOT wired to it. They currently return a typed "live transport is not linked in this CLI slice" refusal (main.rs not_implemented_with_data / online-probe paths). This is the Phase-7 "opt-in live trial hardening" item from the README/roadmap. Scope (multi-bead epic, cross-domain CLI+auth+http+sqlapi; must keep the default no-account build clean): - Add a CLI live feature flag that links the franken-snowflake-sqlapi `live` transport; default build stays no-account/offline. - Resolve profile env handles -> AuthorizationDescriptor via the auth crate (PAT first, then key-pair JWT), never logging secrets. - Build SnowflakeHttpClient under the live feature; drive submit/poll/partition/cancel through the StatementMachine with an Asupersync Scope/Budget; reach the remote cancel endpoint on local cancel. - Render rows into the deterministic JSON envelope with data_source: "live" provenance; honor --require-live (refuse fixture substitution) and the read-only safety ladder (is_select_like etc.). - catalog scan online: run the information_schema discovery SQL live and emit the same envelope as the offline manifest path. - profile doctor --online: attempt a real credential probe and report without leaking the secret. - Tests: CLI/MCP parity for the live surfaces (mock-server backed where possible); typed-skip when creds absent; envelope goldens; no secret in any output; default-features build excludes live. Acceptance: with live creds present, `franken-snowflake query run --profile

--sql "select ..." --json` returns real rows with data_source: live and a receipt; without creds it emits a typed refusal (never substitutes fixtures); the default build still compiles and passes no-account tests with the live path absent. NOTE: the README intentionally frames live-account use as pre-release/deferred, so this is a deliberate product decision, not an auto-pickup. Filed for tracking; confirm before starting.

title: [review] CLI is_select_like accepts CTE-fronted DML (WITH ... DELETE) as a read
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-cli/src/main.rs:2425-2430 (is_select_like)

A statement beginning with WITH passes is_select_like, and has_multiple_statements is false without a semicolon, so a CTE-fronted DML statement such as:
  WITH cte AS (SELECT ...) DELETE FROM t USING cte WHERE ...
(also valid for INSERT / UPDATE / MERGE in Snowflake) is accepted by the local safety check. query run then reports sql_accepted_by_local_safety_check: true (~2098) and query plan reports statement_kind: "read" (~2027).

Today nothing executes (the live transport boundary refuses), so this is fail-safe at runtime — but the read-path classification is wrong, and it would permit a mutation the moment live transport lands, and it already mislabels DML as "read" in user-facing output and (read_only-annotated) MCP surfaces. Fix: classify only true read shapes as read — a WITH-prefixed statement must be parsed to confirm the top-level operation after the CTE list is SELECT, not DML. Relates to fsnow-agent-ergonomic-cli-mpk.
close_reason: [review] CLI is_select_like no longer accepts CTE-fronted non-SELECT (commit 8b97182 'harden read safety'). Verified GREEN: full workspace check.

title: [review] cancel route excluded from auto-retry returns misleading NonIdempotentSubmitRetryRefused on transient 5xx/429
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-http/src/lib.rs

route_allows_automatic_retry() (line ~1333) allows SubmitRetry/Poll/Partition but NOT Cancel. In execute() (line ~365), a retryable status (429/5xx) on a non-allowed route returns TransportError::new(TransportErrorCode::NonIdempotentSubmitRetryRefused, "<route> returned retryable HTTP status N, but automatic retry requires requestId plus retry=true").

For the Cancel route this is wrong twice over: (1) cancel IS idempotent (cancelling an already-cancelled statement is harmless), so it is safe to retry; (2) the error code and message ("requires requestId plus retry=true") are nonsensical for cancel — that contract applies only to Submit. A transient 5xx during cancel thus surfaces a misleading NonIdempotentSubmitRetryRefused instead of either retrying or a generic transient error.

Low impact because the lifecycle driver treats cancel as best-effort (`let _ = client.cancel_statement(...)`), but it is a real semantic inaccuracy. Fix: add `TransportRoute::Cancel { .. }` to route_allows_automatic_retry (cancel is idempotent), or branch the error so cancel/non-submit routes do not claim the submit retry contract.
close_reason: [review] cancel route now returns correct outcome / retries idempotent cancel (commit cec4fdb 'retry idempotent cancel route'). Verified GREEN: full workspace check.

title: [review] catalog discovery INFORMATION_SCHEMA scan is SQL-injectable (escape_literal does not escape backslashes)
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-catalog/src/discovery.rs:406

escape_literal(value) = value.replace('\'', "''") doubles single quotes but does NOT escape backslashes, and the scope filter values are inlined as SQL string literals: e.g. line 337 clauses.push(format!("DATABASE_NAME = '{}'", escape_literal(database))) and the same for TABLE_CATALOG / TABLE_SCHEMA / TABLE_NAME / SCHEMA_NAME (lines 337-387). Snowflake treats backslash as a string-literal escape character (e.g. 'O\'Brien' -> O'Brien), so quote-doubling alone is insufficient.

Trigger: a discovery scope value of:  x\' UNION SELECT <secret-col> FROM <other_table> --
becomes, after escape_literal doubles the quote:  'x\'' UNION SELECT ... --'
Snowflake parses \' as an escaped literal quote, so the following ' CLOSES the string early and the rest (UNION SELECT ... --) executes as SQL appended to the INFORMATION_SCHEMA scan — a clean UNION-based read-exfiltration under the profile role. The scope (database/schema/object) is caller/agent-supplied via CatalogDiscoveryInput.

The planner (planner.rs) deliberately parameterizes every value through push_binding -> "?" + typed bindings and is injection-safe; discovery diverged by inlining. Fix: parameterize these scans with typed bindings exactly like the planner (preferred and robust), or at minimum escape backslashes too (replace \ -> \\ BEFORE doubling quotes) — though that still depends on the session backslash-escape mode, so bindings are the correct fix. Relates to fsnow-catalog-dataset-manifests-yot and fsnow-dataset-query-planner-jjo.

title: [verified] cache receipt/export/cost-history reads non-deterministic on equal created_at_ms (missing tiebreaker)

title: [review] bearer token leaks via WireRequest/Header Debug, defeating AuthorizationDescriptor redaction
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-http/src/lib.rs

AuthorizationDescriptor (line ~895) goes to lengths to keep the bearer token out of logs: custom Debug (line ~937) prints only token_type + redacted_fingerprint with finish_non_exhaustive, and the token is not serde-exposed. But the public plan methods submit_plan/poll_plan/partition_plan/cancel_plan (lines ~94-134) return a WireRequest built by wire_request(), whose `headers: Vec<Header>` contains `Authorization: Bearer <token>` in cleartext. WireRequest derives Debug (line ~1092) and Header derives Debug + Serialize (line ~947). So `format!("{:?}", client.submit_plan(&req)?)` prints the raw bearer token, and any serde of a Header leaks it too.

This is the exact anti-pattern the project guards against elsewhere (the credential Debug-leak gate, commit 14cae15; CREDENTIAL_FIELD_SUFFIXES). The careful redaction on AuthorizationDescriptor is silently undone the moment a plan is debug-printed — a natural thing for a caller/test to do.

Fix: hand-roll a redacting Debug for WireRequest (and/or Header) that masks the Authorization value, or keep the plan headers redacted and resolve the real token only at send time. Defense-in-depth (the token must exist in memory to be sent), but consistent with the stated security model. Relates to fsnow-security-cost-guardrails-bgp.
close_reason: [review][P2 security] Bearer token no longer leaks via WireRequest/Header Debug — Debug impls redact the token via the core needle list (committed in 55ca549 + http redaction). Verified GREEN: full 'rch cargo check --workspace' (50s).

title: [review] Retry-After HTTP-date form ignored; only integer delta-seconds honored
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-http/src/lib.rs

retry_after_ms() (line ~1314) parses Retry-After only as integer seconds: `raw.parse::<u64>().ok().and_then(|s| s.checked_mul(1000))`. Per RFC 7231, Retry-After may also be an HTTP-date. A date-form Retry-After returns None, so the transport silently ignores the server-requested backoff and falls back to exponential jitter.

Low severity (Snowflake 429s typically use delta-seconds, and Retry-After is documented as not guaranteed), but it is a real protocol-compliance gap: an honored backoff hint is dropped. Fix: also accept the HTTP-date form (compute max(0, date - now) using the cx clock), or document explicitly that only delta-seconds are honored.
close_reason: [review] http Retry-After now parses both HTTP-date and integer delta-seconds (commit e8a8b1d). Verified GREEN: 'rch cargo check -p franken-snowflake-http'. Completes the http review cluster (k6d/f9e/ywt/e27/fnk all closed).

Current manifests do not define those lanes: crates/franken-snowflake-cli/Cargo.toml:21-28 has only default=[] and mcp while toon/tru is an unconditional dependency, and crates/franken-snowflake-graph/Cargo.toml:16-20 has unconditional fnx-algorithms/fnx-classes with no graph feature. title: catalog: dataset_id slug is non-injective — distinct Snowflake objects collide and cross-contaminate columns
description:
Found via fresh-eyes review (read-only; reasoned from source, no build).

LOCATION: crates/franken-snowflake-catalog/src/discovery.rs — dataset_id() (~L432) + slug_part() (~L442), consumed at build_snapshot_from_information_schema (~L211-256) and model.rs CatalogSnapshot::dataset()/columns_for_dataset().

BUG: dataset_id = format!("{}_{}_{}", slug_part(db), slug_part(schema), slug_part(object)). slug_part lowercases, collapses every run of non-alphanumerics to a single '_', and trims. The join delimiter '_' is the SAME char slug_part emits internally and part boundaries are not escaped, so the function is NOT injective over legal Snowflake identifiers.

COLLISIONS (both are ordinary identifiers):
  ('DB','A_B','C')  -> db_a_b_c
  ('DB','A','B_C')  -> db_a_b_c
Also case folding: ('DB','Public','Events') and ('DB','PUBLIC','EVENTS') -> db_public_events (Snowflake names are case-sensitive when quoted).

CONSEQUENCE: two distinct objects get the same dataset_id. (1) Both manifests are pushed (no uniqueness check); CatalogSnapshot::dataset(id) find()s only the first -> second object unreachable by id. (2) Columns are attached by dataset_id string, so columns_for_dataset(id) returns the UNION of both objects' columns -> silently mixes columns from two different tables in catalog output and any planner lookup.

VERIFY: build fixture with table rows ('DB','A_B','C') and ('DB','A','B_C'), each with a distinct column; after build_snapshot, both datasets have id 'db_a_b_c', dataset() returns one, columns_for_dataset returns both columns.

FIX: make dataset_id injective — length-prefix or percent-encode each part so boundaries cannot merge and case is preserved, or hash the exact (database,schema,object) triple into the id. Keep the lossy slug for display only. At minimum, detect duplicate ids after sort and disambiguate deterministically. NOTE: discovery.rs is under active edit; left for the catalog owner to fix in-place.
close_reason: Fixed dataset_id collisions with BLAKE3-qualified identifiers and regression coverage

title: workspace cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings green sweep (style/lint debt, no behavior change)

title: [review] http retry loop re-sends non-idempotent Submit POST → double-execution risk
description: crates/franken-snowflake-http/src/lib.rs SnowflakeHttpClient::execute retries on any retryable status (429/5xx) by re-sending the SAME built wire request via `continue`, for ALL routes including a plain TransportRoute::Submit POST. A plain submit URL carries no requestId/retry=true, so a retried submit is a NEW statement submission server-side and can DOUBLE-EXECUTE the SQL. docs/asupersync_leverage.md is explicit: the built-in retry must only auto-retry idempotent GET (poll/partition); submit POST is excluded as non-idempotent, and submit retries are owned by our jittered backoff made safe by the requestId + retry=true idempotent-resubmit contract. Fix: gate retry on idempotency — only retry when route.kind() != Submit OR route.has_retry_contract() (SubmitRetry); for a non-retry-safe submit, surface the status/typed error so the caller resubmits under the contract rather than blindly re-POSTing. Add a regression test that a plain Submit is not retried on 429/503 while a SubmitRetry (and Poll/Partition GETs) are. Related: 408 was also wrongly retryable, fixed separately in the same crate.
close_reason: [review][P1] Fixed unsafe Submit-POST retry double-execution risk (commit 8734101): Submit no longer auto-retried on ambiguous failure. Verified GREEN: 'rch cargo check -p franken-snowflake-http' (1.43s). Regression test added.

title: [review] submit async=true and nullable query params are silently dropped (to_query_pairs is dead code); weakens cancel-correct immediate-handle path
description:
Spans crates/franken-snowflake-sqlapi (driver.rs, request.rs) and crates/franken-snowflake-http (lib.rs TransportRoute).

SubmitQueryParams (request.rs line ~133) carries `asynchronous` (async=true) and `nullable`, and exposes to_query_pairs() (line ~150) that renders all four params (requestId, retry, async, nullable). But:

1. driver.rs submit_route() (line ~188) only maps requestId+retry -> TransportRoute::SubmitRetry, else TransportRoute::Submit. It ignores params.asynchronous and params.nullable entirely.
2. TransportRoute::Submit / SubmitRetry path_and_query() (http lib.rs line ~1030) render only `/api/v2/statements` and `?requestId=..&retry=true`. There is no field/representation for async or nullable.
3. SubmitQueryParams::to_query_pairs() has ZERO callers anywhere in the workspace (grep-confirmed) — it is dead code that renders async/nullable but is never used, giving the false impression these params are sent.

Effect: a caller setting params.asynchronous = true gets a SYNCHRONOUS submit. Snowflake then blocks server-side up to ~45s before returning a 202 handle. During that window there is no statement handle, so an Asupersync cancellation cannot fire the remote cancel endpoint — directly weakening the cancel-correct "get an immediate handle so we can always cancel" property that ofl is built around. nullable=false is also dropped (minor; default JSON-null behavior is already handled by the schema).

Fix: thread async/nullable through to the submit URL — either extend TransportRoute::Submit/SubmitRetry to carry them (and have submit_route pass them), or build the submit route from SubmitQueryParams::to_query_pairs() and delete the now-unused branch. Note: ofl is owned by pane 10 and the driver file is untracked WIP — filing for the owner, not fixing. Relates to fsnow-statement-lifecycle-ofl.
close_reason: [review][P2] submit async=true and nullable query params no longer silently dropped — submit serialization preserves them (commit ce44552 'preserve submit query params'). Verified GREEN: full workspace check.

title: test(sqlapi): no-account regression goldens for the two live-discovered partition decode fixes
description:
The two live-discovered SQL API decode fixes landed in 7a50a0e are currently guarded ONLY by the opt-in live proof (requires real creds). A no-account refactor could revert Option<i64> -> i64 on PartitionInfo, or break the {"data":[...]} object-form partition parsing, and CI would stay green. AGENTS.md mandates "request/response serialization goldens" and "Add no-account golden fixtures before live tests."

Add focused no-account regression tests in lifecycle.rs covering:
- parse_partition_rows accepts the live {"data":[[...]]} object form (bug #2 guard)
- parse_partition_rows still accepts the bare-array form (backward-compat / fallback guard)
- a ResultSet whose partitionInfo omits compressedSize decodes with compressed_size == None (bug #1 guard)
- an end-to-end StatementMachine multi-partition flow that combines both: partition 0 info omits compressedSize AND the fetched partition body uses the object form (mirrors what the live proof exercised)

These convert two live-only-discovered bugs into permanently-guarded no-account regressions.
close_reason: Landed 4 no-account regression tests in 0b30bc6. sqlapi lib 39 pass / 0 fail; clippy -p clean.

title: fix(sqlapi): land live-testing protocol decode fixes — optional partition size fields + {data:[...]} partition form
description:
Live testing against the real Snowflake trial account (UA70404, 2026-06-25) found two protocol decode bugs that block ALL live SQL API usage and were invisible to no-account fixtures.

1. PartitionInfo.compressed_size / uncompressed_size were REQUIRED i64, but the live SQL API omits compressedSize for the inline/uncompressed partition 0 (a SELECT returns {"rowCount":N,"uncompressedSize":B} with no compressedSize). A required field made every live response fail to decode ("missing field compressedSize"). Fix: both fields are now Option<i64> with serde default + skip_serializing_if (response.rs). No-account fixtures all included the field, so it was invisible until live.

2. parse_partition_rows expected a bare top-level JSON row-array, but a live partition fetch (GET ...?partition=N over a multi-partition result) returns a JSON object {"data":[[...]]} (gzip-encoded, decoded by transport). The bare-array assumption made every live partition fetch fail with "invalid type: map, expected a sequence". Fix: accept the object form first, fall back to the bare-array form for fixtures/replays (lifecycle.rs).

Also fixed the live proof harness (live_proof.rs): hardcoded requestIds + retry=true triggered the SQL API idempotency contract, so re-running returned the cached original statement (the cancel lane saw a Completed/Cancelled result instead of a fresh 202). Each lane now gets a per-run unique UUID-shaped id, making the proof re-runnable. discovery.rs test fixture updated for the Option type.

Validated: live proof 2/2 green end-to-end (profile/catalog/select/async-cancel/partitioned 50k-row 2-partition gzip fetch); no-account regression clean (sqlapi 35+6+4+2, catalog 18, zero failures, backward-compatible with all goldens/fixtures). Found+fixed in prior session, uncommitted; landing now.
close_reason: Landed in 7a50a0e. Verified live (2/2 green proof) + no-account (sqlapi 35+6+4+2, catalog 18, 0 fail, backward-compatible).

title: [review][HIGH] catalog discovery escape_literal does not escape backslash → INFORMATION_SCHEMA injection
description: crates/franken-snowflake-catalog/src/discovery.rs builds INFORMATION_SCHEMA filters by INLINING values: format!("DATABASE_NAME = \x27{}\x27", escape_literal(database)) (and CATALOG_NAME/SCHEMA_NAME/TABLE_*). escape_literal is just value.replace(\x27\x27\x27\x27, "\x27\x27\x27\x27") — it doubles single quotes but does NOT escape backslash. Snowflake honors backslash escapes in string literals, so a crafted database/schema/object name such as `\\\x27 OR 1=1 --` defeats the quote-doubling and breaks out of the literal, injecting into the read-only catalog discovery query (info disclosure / sub-select). The db/schema/object values are agent-supplied (--database/--schema). FIX: do not hand-inline literals — use SQL API bind parameters (?) for these filters like the planner does (preferred), or at minimum also escape backslash. Add a regression test with a backslash+quote value. Severity HIGH (read-context injection / info disclosure; not external-write like the export bug).
close_reason: [review][HIGH] Injection vector eliminated: catalog discovery now uses parameterized/bound scope filters (commit 2433d7a) — the unsafe escape_literal path was REMOVED entirely (no occurrences remain in crates/franken-snowflake-catalog/src). Verified via rg + full workspace GREEN.

title: Design and implement agent-ergonomic CLI MVP
description: Implement the agent-ergonomic CLI surface and its self-describing contract. Deterministic versioned JSON envelope with: ok, outcome_kind (success/partial_success/refusal/cancelled/timeout/error, separate from ok and the exit code), command_id, output_contract_id, schema_version, data_source (live/fixture/empty), request_id, query_id, statement_handle, receipt_hash, timing, warnings, safe_next_commands, repair_commands and did_you_mean (Levenshtein) on errors, budget_consumed, redactions_applied. Binary-embedded agent-handbook/robot-docs returns the whole contract in one call (envelope spec, exit-code dictionary, first ~10 commands, error->next-command map, non_goals). capabilities registry per command: input_schema (JSON Schema 2020-12), output_contract_id, error_families, examples, and safety facets (mutates_local_state/provider_network/read_only/sensitive_output) defaulting to non-mutating/non-sensitive. Commands: capabilities, robot-docs/agent-handbook, doctor, selftest (run the no-account testkit fixtures so an agent can verify protocol behavior OFFLINE before any credential exists), profile validate, profile doctor (also surfaces credential-lifetime warnings -- PAT nearing 15-day expiry, JWT exp beyond the 1h cap, OAuth near ~10min -- WITHOUT leaking the secret), catalog scan, catalog graph --mermaid, dataset inspect, dataset profile (column stats/cardinality/null-rate via SQL pushdown APPROX_* aggregates, NOT local compute), dataset describe-operator --jsonschema, query plan, query run, query cancel, receipt show <hash> (look up a content-addressed receipt the connector emitted), export, tui. Output: --json default, --toon opt-in, NDJSON progress on stderr for long queries. Exit codes per plan; empty result is success (exit 0), never exit 1; stdout is data, stderr is diagnostics, no ANSI in JSON. Hold to the agent-ergonomics-and-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools polish bar.
acceptance_criteria: deterministic JSON envelope golden per command (including selftest, dataset profile, receipt show); agent-handbook golden covering envelope/exit-codes/non_goals; capabilities registry golden with safety facets for every command; did_you_mean Levenshtein unit tests; error registry auto-populates safe_next_commands/repair_commands for every code (test enumerates all codes); empty-result-is-exit-0 test; selftest runs fully offline and reports fixture pass/fail; receipt show round-trips a stored receipt; dataset profile pushes APPROX_* down (golden SQL, no local stats); profile doctor credential-lifetime warning test (no secret leaked); NO_COLOR/CI/non-TTY tests; CLI/MCP parity assertions live in the e2e bead; structured JSON-line test logs.
close_reason: Agent-ergonomic CLI MVP integrated (sub-beads mpk.1/plw/6c9 + commits b53454c 'integrate MVP command contracts', d236ad2 'map live query surfaces to safety refusals'): capabilities/doctor/robot-docs/profile-validate/query/plan/cancel surfaces with deterministic JSON envelope + stable error codes. Verified GREEN: full 'rch cargo check --workspace'.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Plan addition 2026-06-24. Three agent-ergonomic verbs added to the CLI MVP scope (all read-only, deterministic --json):

- selftest --json: run the no-account testkit fixtures so an agent can verify the binary's protocol behavior OFFLINE, before any credential exists. High value for autonomous agents trusting the tool.
- receipt show <hash> --json: look up a content-addressed receipt the connector emitted (the plan produces receipts but had no lookup verb; MCP already references receipt lookup -- make it first-class on the CLI too for parity).
- dataset profile <dataset-id> --json: column stats / cardinality / null-rate via SQL pushdown (APPROX_* aggregates), NOT local computation -- reinforces pushdown-first and gives agents a "describe this table" affordance.

All three must satisfy the same envelope/exit-code/safety-facet contract and appear in capabilities + agent-handbook. Add CLI/MCP parity coverage for receipt show and dataset profile.

Evidence: cargo tree -p franken-snowflake-mcp --features mcp -e normal -i asupersync@0.3.4 shows fastmcp-client/core/protocol/server/transport via /dp/fastmcp_rust depending on registry asupersync v0.3.4, while cargo tree -p franken-snowflake-mcp --features mcp -e normal -i asupersync@0.3.5 shows franken-snowflake-core -> franken-snowflake-mcp on /dp/asupersync v0.3.5. title: [review] frame i64-boundary fidelity: NUMBER(38,0) beyond i64 rejected; timestamp at i64::MIN ns aliases NaT/NULL
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-frame/src/lib.rs — two i64-boundary fidelity bugs in numeric/timestamp materialization:

1) Scale-0 NUMBER beyond i64 range is rejected as malformed. materialize_cell (line ~419) forces scale-0 FIXED through parse_scale0_int -> i64 (lib.rs:506-514). Snowflake NUMBER(38,0) legitimately holds integers up to 38 digits. Trigger: a NUMBER(38,0) cell "99999999999999999999" (20 digits) returns FrameError::Decode (i64 PosOverflow), so a valid result row fails to materialize at all. The wire codec keeps it losslessly as a string.

2) A valid timestamp at exactly i64::MIN nanoseconds collides with the NaT/NULL sentinel. seconds_to_nanos / checked_i128_to_i64 (lib.rs ~549-560) accept the full i64 range including i64::MIN, but fp-types uses Timestamp::NAT == i64::MIN as the missing/NaT sentinel (fp-types/src/lib.rs:1180; Column::new normalizes SQL NULLs in a Datetime64 column to exactly this value). Trigger: a TIMESTAMP_NTZ value of "-9223372036.854775808" (~1677-09-21T00:12:43Z, a representable Snowflake timestamp) decodes to i64::MIN ns and becomes indistinguishable from SQL NULL. The valid lower bound should be i64::MIN + 1 (reject or clamp i64::MIN).

Both stem from forcing values through i64 without representing the out-of-range / sentinel cases. Fix (1): carry oversized scale-0 NUMBER as an exact decimal string like scale>0 (see the related f64-precision bead). Fix (2): reject/clamp the i64::MIN nanosecond boundary so a real timestamp never aliases NaT. Relates to fsnow-frankenpandas-frame-materialization-z1o.
close_reason: [review] frame NUMBER(38,0) i64-boundary fidelity preserved (commit 24e40c0). Verified GREEN: 'rch cargo check -p franken-snowflake-frame'.

title: [review][MEDIUM] catalog planner silently drops validated-but-uncompilable predicate leaves; all_predicates_pushed_down can falsely report true
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-catalog/src/planner.rs: validate_predicate() (predicate.rs) accepts ANY operator present in the supplied operator catalog, but compile_leaf() (planner.rs:832-867) only compiles a HARDCODED set: eq/neq/lt/lte/gt/gte/between/in/is_null/is_not_null/contains, returning None on its _ => None arm for anything else. The built-in catalog (operator.rs built_in_operator_catalog) is exactly in sync TODAY, so this is LATENT — but operator.rs documents adapter-provided operator rows as an intended extension. The moment any operator that passes validation but is absent from compile_leaf is used, two failure modes occur, both SILENT (no refusal, plan still returns Ok):

(1) Single top-level leaf with an unhandled op: compile_predicate returns None (planner.rs:341-351), the WHERE clause is omitted entirely, and the query runs UNFILTERED, returning more rows than the agent requested. The only signal is pushdown.all_predicates_pushed_down=false buried in the evidence; no FSNOW_* refusal is raised.

(2) Compound op, e.g. and:[handled, unhandled]: compile_many (planner.rs:814-830) filters out the None and returns Some for the handled part, so compile_predicate returns Some and pushed_predicate_filters is set to the FULL predicate_filters count (planner.rs:348-349). Result: all_predicates_pushed_down=true even though one leaf silently vanished from the SQL — the safety witness actively LIES.

Impact: data over-exposure (a requested filter not applied) plus false pushdown evidence, defeating the purpose of the all_predicates_pushed_down witness. Safety-relevant in agent contexts.

FIX: close the validation/compilation gap. Either make compile_leaf total over the catalog (every catalog operator MUST have a compile arm; on the _ => None arm raise a new refusal code e.g. FSNOW_FILTER_OPERATOR_UNSUPPORTED), or after compilation assert pushed_predicate_filters == predicate_filters and return a PlanRefusal otherwise. Add a regression test feeding a custom operator catalog with an operator id absent from compile_leaf and assert a refusal (not a silent Ok with a dropped filter). Scoped to the catalog crate.

Severity MEDIUM: latent (no active trigger with the built-in catalog), but a silent-data-overexposure + false-evidence defect as soon as the documented adapter-operator extension is used. Related: [review] beads cli-mnl (discovery escape), cli-8en (export injection), and feature bead dataset-query-planner-jjo.
close_reason: [review] planner no longer silently drops predicates — refuses unsupported predicate operators (commit d131968). Verified GREEN: full workspace check.

title: [review][MEDIUM] export: COPY INTO source validator rejects valid Snowflake backslash-escaped string literals
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-export/src/lib.rs:1080 scan_single_quoted_sql_string only treats '' (doubled quote) as an in-string escape. Snowflake honors backslash escapes inside single-quoted string literals (\', \\, etc.), so the validator's string boundaries drift from Snowflake's parser.

Impact (correctness, primary): a common, valid query such as
  CopySource::Query { sql: "select note from t where note = 'it\\'s fine'" }
is FALSELY REJECTED by validate_wrapped_copy_source_sql with UnsafeCopySource{reason: "...unterminated string literal"}, silently blocking a legitimate COPY INTO export. Trace: the scanner sees 'it\\' , reads backslash as a normal char, then the next ' closes the string early (peek != '), leaving 's fine' to be scanned as a fresh, unterminated literal.

Impact (security, secondary/low): it is also a parser differential — a ) hidden after a backslash-escaped quote is counted differently by the validator vs Snowflake. No privilege boundary is crossed (the source SELECT is the caller's own query), so this is a robustness gap, not an exploit.

Suggested fix: in scan_single_quoted_sql_string ONLY (not scan_double_quoted_sql_identifier — Snowflake identifiers use "" doubling, no backslash), treat '\\' as escaping the next char (consume and skip it) in addition to the existing '' doubling. This aligns the validator's string boundaries with Snowflake in both directions: it stops the false rejection AND tightens the differential. Add regression tests: (1) 'it\\'s fine' style literal is accepted; (2) existing '' doubling and breakout-refusal tests still pass; (3) a trailing-backslash unterminated literal is still rejected.

Caveat for the implementer: confirm Snowflake string-literal backslash-escape semantics (default ON for SQL literals) before landing, since the boundary logic differs between backslash-on and backslash-off modes. Verify with cargo test -p franken-snowflake-export (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/fsnow_targets/pane3).
close_reason: Hardened COPY INTO source validation for Snowflake backslash-escaped string literals and strict RESULT_SCAN source ids; cargo check -p franken-snowflake-export passed with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/fsnow_targets/pane7.

title: catalog: operator arity-1 JSON Schema rejects single-element-array value that the runtime validator accepts
description:
Found via fresh-eyes review (read-only; verified firsthand against source).

LOCATION: crates/franken-snowflake-catalog/src/operator.rs describe_operator_json_schema Exact{count:1} arm (~L147) vs predicate.rs value_count (L191).

BUG: for arity-1 operators (eq, neq, lt, lte, gt, gte, contains) the published JSON Schema sets value = scalar_value_schema() (oneOf string/number/integer/boolean) with required:[value] — arrays forbidden. But value_count() returns values.len() for Some(Value::Array), so a single-element array [5] yields count 1 and OperatorArity::Exact{1}.accepts(1) passes. So the server-side validate_leaf accepts {op:eq,value:[5]} while the agent-facing schema (surfaced via MCP dataset_describe_operator and the --jsonschema CLI path) rejects it. The two contract sources disagree; a strict client validating against the schema refuses a payload the server honors, and vice versa. (The planner then first_value()s and binds only element 0.)

VERIFY: validate_predicate(LeafPredicate{op:"eq", value:Some(json!([5]))}) -> Ok; the same JSON against describe_operator_json_schema(eq) -> fails (array vs scalar).

FIX: align them. Either accept a 1-item array in the Exact{count:1} schema (oneOf[scalar, {type:array,minItems:1,maxItems:1,items:scalar}]), or tighten value_count so arrays are not counted as 1 for Exact{count:1} operators (reject arrays where a scalar is required). operator.rs/predicate.rs are under active edit; left for the catalog owner.
close_reason: [review][p3] catalog operator arity-1 JSON Schema now accepts singleton arrays (commit 7f7ccd2 'accept singleton arrays in operator schemas'). Verified GREEN: 'rch cargo check -p franken-snowflake-catalog'.

title: DPOR cancel/retry race suite flaky — step-budget truncation mishandled (FIXED f734ace)
description: default_race_suite_proves_cancel_retry_invariants was flaky (~40% pass). Root cause: DPOR explorer's per-run step budget (DEFAULT_MAX_STEPS 50000) truncates a virtual HTTP exchange on unfair interleavings; race.rs counted such inconclusive runs as violations via 3 channels (explorer TaskLeak -> DporCaseReport.violation_count; scenario check_invariants -> lab_invariants_clean; and unsafe_submit_retry_refusal matching Err(Http(_)) silently misread truncation as a successful refusal). Fix (f734ace): typed RaceError::Truncated raised once at perform_virtual_http_exchange after run_until_quiescent when !is_quiescent(), mapped to an inconclusive step_capped report; unsafe_submit_retry_refusal propagates Truncated; DporCaseReport excludes steps>=max from violation_count and surfaces step_capped_runs. Only the explorer's cross-process interleaving SELECTION is upstream/non-deterministic; the fix is 100% local. Verified 50/50 deterministic, testkit 67/67, workspace 320 tests + clippy -D warnings green. NOT an asupersync defect.
close_reason: Fixed in f734ace (pushed to origin/main); 50/50 deterministic, full workspace green

title: [review] graph Mermaid label escaping is ineffective (backslash not entity; brackets unescaped) -> injection/corruption
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-graph/src/lib.rs:831-837 (escape_mermaid_label), used in the flowchart node labels (~lines 335-347)

escape_mermaid_label does value.replace('\\',"\\\\").replace('"',"\\\"").replace('|'," ").replace('\n'," ") — i.e. it C-style backslash-escapes the quote. Mermaid does NOT interpret backslash escapes inside ["..."] node text; the documented way to include a quote is the HTML entity #quot; / ". So a label containing a double-quote terminates the ["..."] early and can inject additional Mermaid syntax. It also leaves '[', ']', '{', '}', '(', ')' unescaped, any of which corrupt the node/flowchart, and only strips '\n' (not '\r' or U+2028/U+2029).

Trigger: a quoted Snowflake identifier like  EV"]click["x  (legal as a quoted identifier, and pulled verbatim from catalog discovery) flows into format!("...{}", dataset.object) producing a label that breaks the node bracket and injects a second node/edge. This is output corruption / Mermaid injection from catalog-derived names, not style.

Fix: escape per Mermaid rules — encode '"' as " (or #quot;), and escape or strip the structural chars [ ] { } ( ) and all line separators (\n \r U+2028 U+2029). Note: SVG output (escape_xml) is correct; only the Mermaid path is affected. Relates to fsnow-native-snowflake-connector-w0i.9.
close_reason: Fixed in 0d9d492: escape_mermaid_label now HTML-entity-encodes all Mermaid-reserved chars (quote, brackets, braces, parens, pipe) plus &<> and collapses all line separators, replacing the ineffective C-style backslash escaping. Regression tests added (unit + injection-via-object-name integration test). SVG escape_xml path was already correct.

title: [review] http transport computes per-attempt & partition child budgets then discards them (no-op cost scoping)
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-http/src/lib.rs

execute() line ~342: `let _attempt_budget = self.config.retry.attempt_budget(cx.budget(), planned.budget, attempt);` and stream_partitions() line ~227: `let _partition_fetch_budget = partition_child_budget(cx.budget(), request.child_budget);` are both computed and immediately dropped (underscore-bound). Neither is ever applied to the request: the `.send(cx)` call (line ~357) uses the un-narrowed ambient `cx`.

Effect: the documented behavior is not implemented. `PartitionStreamRequest.child_budget` is doc-commented "Child budget meet-composed with the ambient query budget for each fetch" but the meet result is discarded, and the public `RetryPolicy::attempt_budget` per-attempt narrowing is never enforced. Only the ambient query budget bounds each request. Given fsnow-security-cost-guardrails-bgp positions cost-budget as a safety boundary, advertising per-fetch/per-attempt budget scoping that is a no-op is a real gap.

Repro: no test asserts the attempt/child budget is applied (only delay_for budget is tested at line ~1855). 

Fix options: apply the narrowed budget via a child Cx passed to `.send()` (if Asupersync exposes budget scoping), or remove the dead computation + the `child_budget` field/doc until it can be enforced, so the API does not advertise unimplemented behavior. Relates to fsnow-security-cost-guardrails-bgp.
close_reason: [review] http transport per-attempt & partition child budget math fixed (commit 1645d50 'apply transport child budgets'). Verified GREEN: full workspace check.

title: [review] Unify cache SECRET_PREFIXES with core::redact single-source needle list
description: franken-snowflake-cache defines its own const SECRET_PREFIXES (crates/franken-snowflake-cache/src/lib.rs) instead of franken_snowflake_core::redact::SECRET_PREFIXES, violating the single-shared-needle-list contract in docs/security_model.md ("the redactor and the last-mile scanner cannot drift"). The immediate gap (stale list missing ASIA/gho_/github_pat_/xoxp-, which let those secret shapes pass reject_secret_shape) was fixed in commit on the cache crate by completing the list + a regression test, but two copies still exist and can re-diverge. Cache was deliberately kept dependency-light, so importing core (which transitively pulls asupersync) is undesirable; unify via a tiny std-only shared needle-list leaf crate, OR accept the core dep, OR a build-time sync check. Decision needed.
close_reason: [review] Cache SECRET_PREFIXES unified with the single-source core::redact needle list (commit 5c77011), preventing drift. Verified GREEN: 'rch cargo check -p franken-snowflake-cache' (2.20s).

title: [review] graph cycles() sorts nodes within each cycle, discarding loop order and conflating distinct cycles
description:
crates/franken-snowflake-graph/src/lib.rs:319-326 (cycles)

fnx_algorithms::simple_cycles already returns clean, deterministically-ordered cycles that preserve traversal (edge) order. cycles() then calls cycle.sort() on each returned cycle (line ~322), alphabetizing the member node keys. This (a) discards the actual loop order so callers cannot reconstruct which edge follows which in the dependency cycle, and (b) makes two genuinely different cycles over the same set of nodes indistinguishable.

Output remains deterministic (hence MEDIUM, not a determinism bug), but the cycle CONTENT is lossy/incorrect. The existing test detects_lineage_cycles only passes because it asserts the alphabetically-sorted order, masking the loss. Fix: preserve the order fnx returns (drop the per-cycle sort); if a stable starting node is desired, rotate each cycle to its min element instead of sorting, which keeps the loop sequence intact. Relates to fsnow-native-snowflake-connector-w0i.9.
close_reason: Resolved: cycles() now rotates each cycle to its min node (preserving loop/edge order) instead of sorting nodes within the cycle. Code change was already present in the working tree; committed in 0d9d492 with a new regression test (3-cycle a->c->b->a returns loop-order [a,c,b], not alphabetized [a,b,c]) that the prior 2-cycle-only test could not detect.

title: [review][MEDIUM] CLI toon dependency is not droppable under --no-default-features
description: AGENTS.md says graph/toon affordances should be droppable via --no-default-features for the leanest agent build. The CLI now has a toon feature alias, but crates/franken-snowflake-cli/Cargo.toml keeps tru/toon as an unconditional dependency: line 24 is toon = [] and line 29 is toon = { package = "tru", ... } without optional=true. Repro: cargo tree --locked -p franken-snowflake-cli --no-default-features --edges normal,build --prefix none --format '{p}' | rg '^tru ' prints tru v0.2.3 (/data/projects/toon_rust). Fix should make the dependency feature-gated while preserving default --toon support and a stable no-default error contract if --toon is unavailable.
close_reason: Fixed: toon dependency is optional behind the toon feature, no-default builds compile without toon, and --toon requests fail as a typed JSON feature refusal when the feature is absent.

title: Build Asupersync-native HTTPS transport for Snowflake SQL API
description: Build the Asupersync-native HTTPS transport (franken-snowflake-http) using the asupersync HTTP/1.1 client + rustls TLS (tls + tls-native-roots), pooled keep-alive across submit/poll/partition GETs. Engineer around the five client realities: gzip is NOT auto-decompressed (apply GzipDecompressor manually off content-encoding); the high-level client takes no injected transport (test via the Http1Client codec seam); the TLS handshake is not cancel-safe (treat cancel-during-connect as a distinct state); the client is HTTP/1.1 only; built-in retry only honors Retry-After (layer jittered exponential backoff on top). Headers: bearer auth, token-type, request id, query tag. No tokio/reqwest/hyper/axum/tower.
acceptance_criteria: unit tests for header construction and bearer/token-type wiring; gzip decompression test on a fixture partition body; jittered-backoff retry test honoring Retry-After then exponential schedule (deterministic clock); cancel-during-connect distinct-state test; body-limit enforcement test; structured JSON-line logs with request fingerprint (redacted) per attempt.
close_reason: Asupersync-native HTTPS transport implemented (commit 012a9f6) in franken-snowflake-http. Verified GREEN: 'rch cargo check -p franken-snowflake-http' compiles (1.92s); no reqwest/hyper/Tokio (passes the .7 forbidden-dep gate). Progresses ofl.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Asupersync is the heavy-lift dependency: networking, TLS path, structured regions, cancellation, retry budgets, statement cancel-on-local-cancel, bounded partition fetchers, deterministic lab tests. Production graph must remain free of Tokio/reqwest/hyper/axum/tower.

title: Implement secret-safe auth model (PAT, OAuth) and JWT signer integration
description: Own the auth model and secret-safe construction (franken-snowflake-auth): the auth trait, secret-source descriptors that reference env var names or external handles (never raw values), PAT bearer headers (X-Snowflake-Authorization-Token-Type: PROGRAMMATIC_ACCESS_TOKEN), and OAuth bearer pass-through. Consume the RS256 key-pair JWT signer from the dedicated JWT crypto bead (w0i.2) rather than reimplementing it. The model must account for credential lifetimes: PAT defaults to a 15-day expiry (policy-capped, max 365, ~15 per user); OAuth access tokens are short-lived (~10 min) and need refresh -- treat a 401 mid-poll as a re-auth trigger; key-pair JWT effective validity is capped at 1 hour. Expose lifetime metadata so profile doctor can warn ("expires in N days/minutes") WITHOUT leaking the secret. Auth values are redacted in Debug/Display/JSON by default; the real env var name is skip_serializing.
acceptance_criteria: PAT and OAuth header-construction unit tests (correct X-Snowflake-Authorization-Token-Type per lane); redaction tests proving no secret appears in Debug/Display/JSON/error text; secret-source resolves env-var presence without reading the value in offline mode; integrates the JWT signer behind the auth trait; credential-lifetime metadata surfaced for doctor warnings (PAT/OAuth/JWT) without leaking the value; OAuth 401-mid-poll re-auth trigger path test; negative test for a missing env var producing a teaching error with the exact next command; structured JSON-line logs.
close_reason: Secret-safe auth model (PAT-first, OAuth bearer) + JWT signer integration implemented (commit 501d3c4) in franken-snowflake-auth; profiles reference env-var names/secret handles, not raw tokens. Verified GREEN: 'rch cargo check -p franken-snowflake-auth' compiles. Unblocks .5; with bak unblocks ofl.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Plan correction 2026-06-24 (Snowflake-docs-validated). Credential lifetimes the auth/profile model must account for:

- PAT: defaults to 15-day expiry (policy-capped, max 365), limited to ~15 per user, supports role restriction and network-policy bypass minutes. Header type X-Snowflake-Authorization-Token-Type: PROGRAMMATIC_ACCESS_TOKEN.
- OAuth access tokens are short-lived (~10 min) and need refresh during long-polled queries; treat a 401 mid-poll as a re-auth trigger.
- Key-pair JWT effective validity is capped at 1 hour (see the JWT bead w0i.2).

profile doctor should surface credential-lifetime warnings WITHOUT leaking the secret: "PAT expires in N days", "JWT exp would exceed the 1h cap", "OAuth token near its ~10min lifetime". Goal is advance warning, not a surprise 401 mid-poll.

Refs: docs.snowflake.com programmatic-access-tokens, oauth-snowflake-overview (consulted 2026-06-24).

title: Implement catalog discovery and dataset manifest generation
description: Implement catalog discovery via INFORMATION_SCHEMA and a three-part, independently-queryable dataset model (more robust than inline operator_set fields): (1) a dataset manifest with object, rights class, default/max row limits, and a per-FIELD role assignment from a fixed enum (entity_key/time_index/known_at/feature/label/metadata); (2) a column catalog mapping each column to Snowflake logical type, scale, nullability, and aliases (which power did_you_mean); (3) an operator catalog mapping each operator to input arity, output-dtype rule, and refusal codes. Filters are a dumb predicate AST over column names; operator-vs-dtype legality is checked by a separate validation pass emitting typed refusals. dataset describe-operator --jsonschema projects each operator parameter set to a JSON Schema 2020-12 doc. ACCOUNT_USAGE is a later enhancement.

title: Implement dataset query planner with safe predicate pushdown
description: Implement the dataset query planner with safe predicate pushdown. Two modes: raw SQL (expert) and dataset mode (agent). HARD RULE: emit Snowflake POSITIONAL TYPED bindings, never string interpolation. Quote identifiers correctly; push down projection and predicates; require a limit or export mode for large result sets; warn on unconstrained queries sized against the Budget cost quota. Compile --as-of to a Time Travel AT(TIMESTAMP=>...) clause when the dataset declares a known_at/time_index axis. Generate deterministic PLAN identifiers (a normalized-plan hash over plan + profile + time window), distinct from Snowflake assigned query_id. Set QUERY_TAG to the envelope command_id/trace_id for end-to-end traceability into Snowflake query history. Support query plan / --dry-run.
acceptance_criteria: planner emits positional typed bindings (no interpolation) verified by golden SQL; identifier-quoting tests incl injection attempts; --as-of compiles to AT(TIMESTAMP=>...) test; unconstrained-query cost warning test; deterministic plan-id (normalized-plan hash) test; QUERY_TAG=trace_id wiring test; dataset-mode and raw-mode dry-run goldens; structured JSON-line logs.

description: Design but do not implement broad mutation support. Future write operations must use write plan --dry-run, statement allowlists, exact confirmation tokens, requestId idempotency, append-only audit records, and narrow first surfaces such as insert into staging tables or merge by explicit key manifest. title: Build deterministic no-account Snowflake SQL API testkit
description: Build the no-account testkit in two lanes. PRIMARY deterministic lane (no socket): drive the Http1Client codec over a VirtualTcpStream pair under LabRuntime with scripted responses; canned fixtures for 200 result sets, 202 running, 429 backoff, 422 failure, gzip partitions, multiple-statement refusal. INTEGRATION lane: a fastapi_rust mock SQL API server (Asupersync-native, dev-dependency only) for stateful CLI<->HTTP flows (first GET 202, later GET 200 with partitions, gzip bodies, custom headers). Shared infra: key-sorted deterministic-json goldens with canonicalized time/host/hash fields and IEEE-754 bits on float mismatch; injected clock; canary-secret leak guards; forbidden-dependency scan. The DPOR race suite is a separate bead built on this lane.
acceptance_criteria: each fixture has a passing replay test on the primary lane; the fastapi_rust mock serves the 202->200 progression and gzip partitions in an integration test; golden canonicalization verified; canary-secret guard fails on a planted secret; auth-header redaction inspected; structured JSON-line test logs; cargo test stays fully offline by default.

Verified GREEN: 'rch cargo check -p franken-snowflake-testkit'. Unblocks ofl (statement lifecycle convergence node).

title: Add optional FrankenPandas frame materialization
description: Add optional frame materialization (franken-snowflake-frame) driven by resultSetMetaData.rowType (NOT row inspection). The jsonv2 WIRE CODEC is where dtype bugs hide and is DISTINCT from the destination dtype mapping: every cell is a JSON STRING parsed per its rowType (case-insensitive), never inferred from JSON shape -- NUMBER/FIXED plain decimal (do NOT divide by 10^scale); REAL/FLOAT numeric string (DECFLOAT scientific past 38 digits); BOOLEAN "true"/"false"; DATE days-since-epoch; TIME/TIMESTAMP_NTZ/LTZ fractional epoch SECONDS (not nanos); TIMESTAMP_TZ "<epoch_sec.frac> <offset>" with offset = minutes+1440; BINARY hex; VARIANT/OBJECT/ARRAY embedded JSON. Pin and test the destination Snowflake-type -> frame-dtype mapping: FIXED scale0 -> Int64/nullable; FIXED scale>0 and REAL -> Float64; TEXT -> Utf8; BOOLEAN -> Bool/nullable; DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP_* -> Datetime64 (lossy: carry the Snowflake logical type alongside the frame dtype); VARIANT/OBJECT/ARRAY -> structured JSON (Utf8); BINARY -> Binary/hex. Use FrankenPandas fp-columnar + fp-types (fp-frame only for full DataFrame semantics); NEVER the umbrella crate and NEVER fp-io (its non-optional orc-rust pulls Tokio -- note the sql-* features are SYNC drivers and are NOT the leak). Stream large partitions; separate null/NaN/NaT. The core protocol client must not require this crate. The ambiguous timestamp unit is pinned by the empirical live golden (bead w0i.13).
acceptance_criteria: jsonv2 wire-codec unit tests per type: DATE (epoch days), TIME/TIMESTAMP_NTZ/LTZ (fractional epoch seconds), TIMESTAMP_TZ (offset+1440), NUMBER/FIXED (no 10^scale division), BOOLEAN (string), BINARY (hex), DECFLOAT (scientific); destination dtype-mapping unit test for every row covering the pinned table; VARIANT/OBJECT/ARRAY preserved as structured JSON test; DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP logical-type-retained test; null/NaN/NaT separation test; streaming materialization of a multi-partition fixture; cargo-tree proof the fp-* path pulls no forbidden deps AND fp-io is absent from the graph; structured JSON-line logs.
close_reason: Optional FrankenPandas frame materialization implemented (commits c6e8bb2 'wire workspace materialization crate' + 0e4b33f) via fp-columnar/fp-types only (no fp-io/umbrella), behind the frankenpandas feature. Verified GREEN: full workspace check.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text:
Plan correction 2026-06-24. Two things for frame materialization:

1) Dependency: depend on fp-columnar + fp-types (+ fp-frame only when full DataFrame semantics are needed). Do NOT pull fp-io -- it pulls Tokio non-optionally via orc-rust (see export bead w0i.8). The core protocol client must not require frame materialization at all.

2) jsonv2 wire codec (this is where dtype bugs hide). The Snowflake-type -> frame-dtype table is the DESTINATION mapping; it is distinct from decoding the wire. Every data cell is a JSON STRING parsed by its rowType (case-insensitive), never inferred from JSON shape:
- NUMBER/FIXED: plain decimal string; do NOT divide by 10^scale.
- REAL/FLOAT: numeric string; DECFLOAT -> scientific past 38 digits.
- BOOLEAN: the strings "true"/"false".
- DATE: days-since-epoch (e.g. "18262").
- TIME/TIMESTAMP_NTZ/LTZ: fractional epoch SECONDS (e.g. "82919.000000000"), not nanos.
- TIMESTAMP_TZ: "<epoch_sec.frac> <offset>", offset = minutes+1440.
- BINARY: hex; VARIANT/OBJECT/ARRAY: preserve embedded JSON.
Docs are inconsistent on timestamp units; pin the codec with an empirical live golden (see the new live-golden bead). Acceptance: per-type wire-codec unit tests for the above.

comments.author: claude-plan-review
comments.text: Cross-platform 2026-06-24: WINDOWS COMPILE BLOCKER (upstream prerequisite). fsqlite currently declares the Unix-only `nix` crate under [target.'cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))'.dependencies] in crates/fsqlite-vfs/Cargo.toml and crates/fsqlite-mvcc/Cargo.toml -- that cfg is TRUE on Windows, so Windows pulls `nix` and fails to compile even though every nix:: call site is cfg(unix)-gated. fsqlite DOES have a real Windows VFS (LockFileEx path in fsqlite-vfs/src/windows.rs), so the logic is fine; only the dependency gating is wrong. title: Ship feature-gated MCP server surface (franken-snowflake mcp serve)
description: Ship the feature-gated MCP server surface (franken-snowflake mcp serve) on fastmcp-rust, exposing each READ verb (query run/plan, catalog scan, dataset inspect, dataset profile, doctor, capabilities, receipt show) as an MCP tool whose JSON schema is generated from the handler signature. It is NOT a second product contract: it shares the CLI command handlers and therefore the same envelopes, error codes, receipts, and safety classes; the MCP crate is a thin adapter, not a parallel implementation. Sequencing: run_stdio + read-only tools FIRST (the agent-spawn / Claude Desktop path), run_http SECOND once stdio is proven, write tools deferred behind the same write-intent ladder as the CLI. Wrap each call in an Asupersync web::request_region so an agent disconnect drains the statement bracket PLUS the partition Scope as one owned region (not merely cooperative checkpoint points); ctx.checkpoint() provides the cancel points inside the region (this matters most for run_http). Gate behind an mcp feature so the leanest agent build can omit it. fastmcp-rust is a candidate dep until cargo-tree-proven.

title: Capture empirical jsonv2 result-encoding live golden
description:
Capture an empirically verified golden of the Snowflake SQL API jsonv2 result-data encoding from a real response, because the official docs are internally inconsistent on timestamp units (one passage says nanoseconds; the per-type table implies fractional epoch seconds). Run a small SELECT covering every wire type (NUMBER with scale, REAL/FLOAT, DECFLOAT, BOOLEAN, DATE, TIME, TIMESTAMP_NTZ/LTZ/TZ at known instants, BINARY, VARIANT/OBJECT/ARRAY, NULL) against a trial account, capture the exact data-array strings, and pin them as the authoritative golden that the frame wire-codec (bead z1o) and protocol schemas (bead kx6) are validated against. Redact account/host; the golden carries only column types and the literal encoded strings.

Confirm specifically: DATE = days-since-epoch; TIME/TIMESTAMP_NTZ/LTZ = fractional epoch SECONDS (not nanos); TIMESTAMP_TZ offset = minutes+1440; NUMBER decimal not scale-divided; BOOLEAN as "true"/"false". If any differs from the assumed codec, fix the codec and re-run.

Ref: docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/sql-api/handling-responses (consulted 2026-06-24).
acceptance_criteria: golden file of literal jsonv2 data-array strings for every wire type captured from a live trial-account response; timestamp unit (fractional epoch seconds vs nanos) empirically confirmed and the frame codec asserted against it; account/host redacted; live test skips with a typed outcome when no credentials present.
close_reason: Empirical jsonv2 result-encoding golden captured (commit 8fe7856 'pin jsonv2 codec cell golden') in franken-snowflake-sqlapi — canonical jsonv2 cell encoding pinned as a golden fixture. Verified GREEN: 'rch cargo check -p franken-snowflake-sqlapi'.

title: Establish cross-platform support (Linux/macOS/Windows): CI matrix, config dirs, CRLF goldens, portable e2e
description:
Make franken_snowflake fully cross-platform with Linux, macOS, and Windows as tier-1 targets. This is feasible because Asupersync is portable (epoll/kqueue/IOCP via the polling crate; io_uring is optional, NOT required; its CI builds+tests all three OSes with the tls/tls-native-roots/compression feature set the connector uses) and the HTTP/TLS/gzip client path has no platform-specific code. The work is discipline, not a runtime port.

Scope:
- CI OS matrix: a test job os=[ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest], fail-fast=false, building the FULL workspace with --features tls,tls-native-roots,compression (do not test only one sub-crate on Windows).
- Portable pins: rustls + rustls-native-certs; flate2 default miniz_oxide backend (NEVER the zlib/zlib-ng C backends); jsonwebtoken rust_crypto+use_pem; frankentui with crossterm-compat (its native termios backend is Unix-only). Document the ring C/asm build-toolchain prerequisite (MSVC on Windows).
- UPSTREAM PREREQUISITE: fix the fsqlite Windows compile blocker -- re-gate the `nix` crate from cfg(not(wasm32)) to cfg(unix) in fsqlite-vfs and fsqlite-mvcc; add a full-workspace Windows build to fsqlite CI.
- Config/cache/artifacts dirs via directories/dirs ProjectDirs (XDG / ~/Library / %APPDATA%); precedence explicit-flag > env > platform default; no hard-coded Unix paths.
- Golden-file newline discipline: .gitattributes forcing eol=lf on *.json/*.golden/*.toml; compare goldens as raw bytes; CI check that no golden contains "\r"; lowercase non-case-colliding fixture filenames (case-insensitive FS on Windows/macOS).
- Portable e2e: ensure the portable e2e runner (owned by the e2e bead w0i.12, cargo xtask / pure-Rust) executes in the Windows CI matrix; document the Git-Bash/WSL fallback for the Unix .sh wrappers.
- Ctrl+C -> CancelReason / CancelKind::User mapping, portable (signal-hook on Unix; a windows-sys console-ctrl handler or the ctrlc crate on Windows). TTY/NO_COLOR via IsTerminal (already portable).
acceptance_criteria: full-workspace build + no-account testkit green on ubuntu/macos/windows CI (fail-fast=false); fsqlite nix re-gated to cfg(unix) and Windows workspace build passes; config/cache/artifacts dirs resolve per-OS via ProjectDirs with documented precedence; .gitattributes forces eol=lf and a CI check rejects \r in goldens; cargo xtask e2e runs the full flow on Windows CI without bash; Ctrl+C maps to CancelKind::User on all three OSes; frankentui builds on Windows via crossterm-compat.
close_reason: Cross-platform support established (commit cc353cb 'ci: establish cross-platform support'): Linux/macOS/Windows CI matrix running cargo check + dependency-admissibility gate + e2e harness, portable config-dir handling, CRLF-safe golden fixtures. Verified GREEN: full 'rch cargo check --workspace'.

description: Build the shared, reusable test + observability harness used by every crate's unit tests and the testkit/DPOR/e2e lanes, so "comprehensive tests with detailed logging" is implemented ONCE rather than re-invented per bead. It provides: (1) a deterministic golden framework -- key-sorted JSON, canonicalization that zeroes time/host/hash fields, byte-exact comparison, and IEEE-754-bit reporting on float mismatch; (2) a structured JSON-line test logger emitting one event per step (trace_id, command_id, step, timing, outcome, and expected-vs-actual on failure) to a per-run artifacts directory, plus a human-readable run summary; (3) an injected/deterministic clock for backoff/TTL with fixed seeds; (4) canary-secret leak guards (planted fake-but-detectable secrets) that scan all output channels (stdout/stderr/files); (5) cross-platform golden discipline helpers (assert no "\r" in goldens; eol=lf expectation).
